summary: Matched a large portion of Dolphin DVD code by replacing most of asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s with C in src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c. The PR notes the implementation was "Modified from prime decomp". It added DVD state-machine code, callbacks, cancellation/error handling, reset/cover-close logic, low-level DVD command dispatch, and supporting header definitions such as OSAlarm, DVDDriveInfo, DVDReset, and OSPhysicalToCached. Review evidence is minimal: one approval with no body and no inline comments.

key_files.role: Removed most hand-written/disassembled DVD assembly; diff shows 2667 deleted lines and only a small residual assembly section.
Added the C implementation for the DVD state machine and public/internal DVD control functions.
New private header containing forward declarations for static DVD callback/state functions needed to order the C implementation.
New OSAlarm type/header with OSAlarmHandler and OSAlarm layout used by DVD cover-close reset timing.
Added DVDDriveInfo and DVDReset declaration; cleaned DVDFileInfo typedef/formatting.
Added cached address conversion macros OS_CACHED_REGION_PREFIX, OS_BASE_CACHED, and OSPhysicalToCached.
Changed AlarmHandler from global asm to static asm, likely to avoid symbol conflict with the new DVD-side AlarmHandler.

what_changed: Converted a large DVD assembly block into C: asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s dropped from a 2675-line text section to a small residual section, while src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c gained 1173 lines.

Added DVD runtime state globals in dvd.c, including executing, IDShouldBe, bootInfo, PauseFlag, PausingFlag, AutoFinishing, FatalErrorFlag, CurrCommand, Canceling, CancelCallback, ResumeFromHere, CancelLastError, LastError, NumInternalRetry, ResetRequired, FirstTimeInBootrom, DVDInitialized, and LastState.

Implemented state/callback flow around stateReadingFST, cbForStateReadingFST, cbForStateError, stateTimeout, stateGettingError, CategorizeError, CheckCancel, cbForStateGettingError, unrecovered-error retry callbacks, stateGoToRetry, stateCheckID variants, stateCoverClosed, stateMotorStopped, stateReady, stateBusy, DVDReset, and DVDCancelAsync.
Modeled boot info as OSBootInfo with magic and fst_start fields, and used bootInfo->magic instead of the earlier boot_magic placeholder.
Added OSAlarm.h and used OSCreateAlarm/OSSetAlarm style reset delay logic in stateCoverClosed, including an internal AlarmHandler that resets DVD and resumes stateCoverClosed_CMD.
Added DVDDriveInfo to the public DVD header and used sizeof(DVDDriveInfo) for the inquiry command path.
Added OSPhysicalToCached macros in os.h to support address conversion needs in the broader DVD implementation.
Made src/dolphin/os/OSSerial.c's asm AlarmHandler static, which avoids exporting a colliding AlarmHandler name after DVD code introduced its own static AlarmHandler.
smart_moves: Reused Prime decomp as a starting point instead of reconstructing the entire DVD state machine manually; the PR body explicitly says it was modified from prime decomp.
Kept the many mutually recursive static state functions manageable by introducing src/dolphin/dvd/dvd_private.h with forward declarations.

Preserved the original DVD state-machine shape: named states and callbacks mirror labels from the removed asm, such as stateReadingFST, cbForStateGettingError, stateGoToRetry, stateMotorStopped, and stateReady.

Encoded hardware/drive error behavior in named helper routines such as CategorizeError, stateError, CheckCancel, and stateTimeout, making previously label-heavy assembly control flow searchable and maintainable.
Matched low-level volatile hardware access explicitly through an extern volatile IO struct at 0xCC006000, including the error register used by cbForStateGettingError.
Resolved symbol hygiene proactively by making OSSerial.c's existing AlarmHandler static when a DVD AlarmHandler was introduced.
decomp_lessons: Large SDK subsystems can often be matched by porting from a sibling decomp, but the imported code still needs local adaptation for headers, symbol visibility, struct names, globals, and project style.
For state-machine decompilation, preserving original state/callback names from assembly labels makes review and future debugging much easier than flattening the logic.
Private forward-declaration headers can be a practical tactic when many static functions are cyclically referenced; this avoids disruptive function reordering solely for C declaration order.
When matching SDK code, public headers may need to grow at the same time as source files: here DVDDriveInfo, DVDReset, OSAlarm, and cached-address macros were prerequisites for the C implementation.
Be alert for accidental global symbol collisions introduced by newly decompiled C functions; marking unrelated asm helpers static can be part of making a new C translation link cleanly.
Volatile hardware register structs and fixed-address externs are preferable to opaque integer loads once the register layout needed by the function is understood.
naming_conventions: DVD state functions use lower camel-case with a state prefix, e.g. stateReadingFST, stateGettingError, stateGoToRetry, stateCoverClosed, stateMotorStopped, stateReady, and stateBusy.
DVD callback functions use cbForState... names, e.g. cbForStateReadingFST, cbForStateGettingError, cbForStateGoToRetry, cbForStateMotorStopped, and cbForStateBusy.

Internal DVD globals in this slice use SDK-like PascalCase or mixed-case names such as DummyCommandBlock, ResetAlarm, LastState, FatalErrorFlag, ResumeFromHere, LastError, NumInternalRetry, and ResetRequired.
Public SDK types and APIs retain Dolphin-style prefixes: DVDCommandBlock, DVDFileInfo, DVDDriveInfo, DVDReset, OSAlarm, OSAlarmHandler, and OSPhysicalToCached.
The imported code uses intType for DVD low-level callback interrupt/status arguments.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The core tactic was asm-to-C replacement: 2667 lines deleted from asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s and 1173 lines added to src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c.
Used small inline helpers, including stateError and CheckCancel, to reproduce recurring assembly sequences without duplicating every callback tail.
Used magic error constants visible in the assembly, including 0x1234567, 0x1234568, 0x20400, 0x62800, 0x23A00, 0xB5A01, and 0x31100, in the C categorization/timeout paths.
Matched low-level branch behavior through explicit intType tests: intType == 16 for timeouts, intType & 1 for success-like paths, and intType & 2 for error-like paths.
Preserved retry/resume behavior through state variables such as LastState, ResumeFromHere, NumInternalRetry, CurrCommand, Canceling, and CancelCallback.
Used fixed-address externs for hardware and OS data, including IO at 0xCC006000 and __OSBusClock at 0x800000F8.
Introduced the public/private header declarations required for the C compiler to accept the new ordering and signatures without leaving assembly labels as the primary source.

review_feedback.note: No issue comments, review comments, or substantive reviewer feedback were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that refined src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c after the Prime decomp import.
Search for subsequent removal of remaining asm/dolphin/dvd/dvd.s residual symbols.
Check whether OSAlarm.h was later expanded with OSCreateAlarm and OSSetAlarm prototypes.
Look for later naming cleanup around DVD private globals such as IO.unk0, IO.unk4, tmpBuffer, and ResetAlarm.
Search for PRs touching DVDCancelAsync or DVD error categorization constants after this match.
